articles.headline: India Unveils 'Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0': Ambitious Targets Set for Energy Transition
articles.explainer:
• The second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish five major Green Hydrogen production hubs across India.
• Targets include achieving 10 million tonnes per annum of Green Hydrogen production capacity by 2030, attracting significant investments, and creating 6 lakh jobs.
• Focus on developing indigenous manufacturing capabilities for electrolysers and associated supply chain components, boosting 'Make in India' in the energy sector.
• New incentives for R&D in green hydrogen technologies and pilot projects for diverse end-use sectors like steel, mobility, and shipping.
• The mission seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy in Landmark Data Protection Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a significant judgment, emphasized the inviolability of the Right to Privacy in the context of emerging data protection challenges.
• The ruling clarified certain ambiguities regarding data sharing between government agencies and private entities, stressing the need for explicit consent.
• It reiterated principles from the K.S. Puttaswamy judgment, affirming privacy as an intrinsic part of Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for the implementation of the proposed Digital Personal Data Protection Bill.
• Calls for a robust regulatory framework that balances national security concerns with individual data autonomy.

articles.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' is an aspect of the Right to Privacy, allowing individuals to request removal of personal data under certain conditions.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Strengthens Indo-Pacific Cooperation, Economic Ties
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue concluded with renewed commitments to bolster comprehensive strategic partnership, focusing on the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions centered on enhancing maritime security cooperation, counter-terrorism efforts, and greater synergy in regional multilateral forums.
• Both sides emphasized accelerating negotiations for a modernized ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) to boost bilateral trade and investment.
• New initiatives were announced for digital connectivity, capacity building, and sustainable development projects in ASEAN member states.
• The dialogue reaffirmed India's 'Act East Policy' and ASEAN's centrality in India's vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate Steady Amidst Inflationary Concerns, Growth Projections
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the benchmark repo rate at 6.5%.
• The decision reflects a cautious stance amidst lingering global inflationary pressures and volatile crude oil prices, despite domestic inflation showing signs of moderation.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth for FY 2025-26 at 7.0%, while retail inflation is expected to average 4.8%.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is prioritizing price stability, waiting for durable signs of disinflation before considering rate cuts.

articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

articles.headline: Indigenous Light Combat Helicopter 'Prachand' Fully Inducted into IAF Fleet
articles.explainer:
• The Light Combat Helicopter (LCH) 'Prachand', developed by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), has been fully inducted into the Indian Air Force (IAF).
• This marks a significant milestone for India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in defense manufacturing and reduces reliance on foreign imports.
• The LCH is an all-weather, agile attack helicopter designed for high-altitude operations, anti-insurgency, and combat search & rescue roles.
• It features advanced stealth characteristics, armored protection, night attack capability, and a robust weapon suite.
• The induction enhances the IAF's combat capabilities, particularly in challenging terrains like the Himalayas, bolstering national security.

articles.headline: Parliament Concludes Monsoon Session: Key Social Welfare Bills Receive Assent
articles.explainer:
• The Monsoon Session of Parliament concluded successfully, witnessing the passage of several important bills aimed at social welfare and governance reforms.
• Among the key legislations passed were amendments to the National Food Security Act (NFSA) to streamline distribution and a new bill on universal healthcare access.
• The session also saw intense debates on issues ranging from economic policies to inter-state water disputes, highlighting the vibrancy of India's parliamentary democracy.
• Despite initial disruptions, the productivity of both Houses remained satisfactory, with significant legislative business being conducted.
• The government emphasized its commitment to inclusive development and strengthening social safety nets through these new laws.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Gen Navigation Satellite NVS-03, Enhancing Regional Positioning
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched NVS-03, a next-generation navigation satellite, aboard the GSLV Mk-II rocket from Sriharikota.
• NVS-03 is part of India's Navigation with Indian Constellation (NavIC) system, designed to provide accurate real-time positioning and timing services over India and its surrounding region.
• This satellite incorporates advanced L1 and L5 band signals, enhancing accuracy and availability for both civilian and strategic users.
• The launch further strengthens India's self-reliance in space-based navigation and reduces dependence on foreign GPS systems.
• NavIC applications include disaster management, vehicle tracking, precise timing, and mapping services, contributing to various socio-economic sectors.

articles.trivia: NavIC was initially approved as the Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System (IRNSS) and provides services in two types: Standard Positioning Service (SPS) for civilian users and Restricted Service (RS) for authorized users.

articles.headline: India's Progress Towards SDGs: Report Identifies Key Challenges and Opportunities
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by NITI Aayog provides an updated assessment of India's progress towards achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by 2030.
• While significant strides have been made in areas like poverty reduction (SDG 1) and access to clean energy (SDG 7), challenges persist in health (SDG 3), education (SDG 4), and gender equality (SDG 5).
• The report highlights regional disparities in SDG achievement, with certain states lagging behind national averages, necessitating targeted interventions.
• It emphasizes the critical role of robust data collection, multi-stakeholder partnerships, and innovative financing mechanisms to accelerate progress.
• Recommendations include strengthening decentralized planning, enhancing social protection, and investing in green technologies to meet the ambitious 2030 agenda.

articles.headline: India and African Nations Forge Alliance on Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• India and a consortium of African nations have announced a new partnership aimed at developing and implementing climate-resilient agricultural practices.
• The initiative focuses on technology transfer, capacity building, and collaborative research in areas like drought-resistant crops, water management, and sustainable farming.
• This alliance strengthens South-South cooperation, leveraging India's expertise in agricultural innovation and climate adaptation.
• Joint projects will include pilot farms, training programs for farmers, and sharing of best practices to enhance food security and rural livelihoods in both regions.
• The partnership underscores a shared commitment to addressing the impacts of climate change on agriculture and promoting sustainable development.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework to Boost Critical Mineral Exploration and Production in India
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines launched a new comprehensive policy framework aimed at accelerating the exploration and production of critical minerals in India.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for private sector participation, advanced geological surveys, and streamlined auction processes for mineral blocks.
• Critical minerals like Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements are crucial for India's energy transition, electric vehicle manufacturing, and high-tech industries.
• The framework emphasizes sustainable mining practices, environmental protection, and ensuring fair compensation for local communities.
• India seeks to reduce its import dependence on these strategic minerals and build a resilient domestic supply chain, bolstering economic security.

articles.headline: Urban Heat Island Effect Intensifies in Major Cities, Posing Public Health Risks
articles.explainer:
• A recent study indicates that the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect is intensifying in major Indian cities, leading to significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas.
• This phenomenon is primarily driven by rapid urbanization, extensive use of heat-absorbing materials (concrete, asphalt), lack of green spaces, and anthropogenic heat emissions.
• The elevated temperatures pose serious public health risks, including heat strokes, respiratory illnesses, and increased energy consumption for cooling.
• Researchers advocate for sustainable urban planning strategies such as increasing green infrastructure, using cool pavements, and implementing urban forestry programs.
• Addressing UHI is crucial for climate resilience, improving urban livability, and achieving India's climate action goals.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Environmental pollution and degradation
Climate Change
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Definition and causes of the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect.

mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the intensification of the Urban Heat Island effect in Indian cities and its environmental consequences.
Discuss the significant public health risks and socio-economic challenges posed by elevated urban temperatures.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various sustainable urban planning strategies and technological solutions in mitigating the UHI effect.
Examine the role of local governance, citizen participation, and inter-agency coordination in building climate-resilient cities.
Suggest comprehensive policy interventions and design guidelines for creating greener and cooler urban environments in India.
articles.imageDescription: Aerial view of a city showing concrete buildings and limited green spaces, with red heat map overlay.
articles.trivia: The temperature difference between urban and rural areas due to the UHI effect can be as much as 10-12°C, especially at night.

articles.headline: ASI Unearths Significant Ancient Buddhist Monastery Complex in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a large, well-preserved ancient Buddhist monastery complex in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The excavation revealed multiple stupas, viharas, a prayer hall, and numerous artifacts including statues of Buddha and Bodhisattvas, indicating a thriving Buddhist center.
• Initial findings suggest the complex dates back to the 7th-10th centuries CE, providing valuable insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India.
• This discovery adds to Odisha's rich Buddhist heritage, alongside sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri, often referred to as the 'Diamond Triangle'.
• The site holds immense potential for historical research, cultural tourism, and understanding ancient trade routes and religious practices.

articles.trivia: The Mahabodhi Temple Complex at Bodh Gaya, Bihar, is one of the four holy sites related to the life of the Lord Buddha and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

articles.headline: Digital India Progress Review: Emphasis on Bridging Rural-Urban Digital Divide
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) conducted a comprehensive review of the Digital India program, focusing on its reach in rural areas.
• The review highlighted significant progress in digital literacy and e-governance service delivery, but underscored the persistent rural-urban digital divide.
• New targets were set for the BharatNet project to ensure broadband connectivity to all Gram Panchayats by 2027, accelerating optical fiber deployment.
• Emphasis was placed on promoting local language content, affordable digital devices, and enhancing digital infrastructure in underserved regions.
• The government reiterated its commitment to financial inclusion, digital empowerment, and equitable access to digital services for all citizens.

articles.headline: Editorial: Navigating the Multipolar World - India's Strategic Imperatives
articles.explainer:
• The global geopolitical landscape is rapidly shifting towards a multipolar order, characterized by multiple centers of power and increasing complexities.
• India's foreign policy must skillfully navigate this evolving environment, balancing traditional alliances with new strategic partnerships.
• The editorial emphasizes the importance of strategic autonomy, economic diplomacy, and strengthening multilateral institutions.
• India's growing economic heft and demographic dividend position it as a crucial player, but also bring greater responsibilities.
• It calls for a proactive and pragmatic approach to safeguard national interests while contributing to global peace and stability.

articles.headline: Government Releases Comprehensive Guidelines for Ethical AI Development and Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released a comprehensive set of guidelines for the ethical development and responsible governance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The guidelines emphasize principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, privacy, and non-discrimination in AI systems.
• They aim to foster innovation in AI while mitigating potential risks such as algorithmic bias, job displacement, and misuse of AI technologies.
• The framework proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for effective AI regulation.
• This move positions India as a leader in developing a balanced approach to AI, promoting its benefits while addressing ethical and societal concerns.

articles.headline: India's First Underwater Metro Expands Operations in Kolkata
articles.explainer:

• The extended route will connect more key areas of the city, significantly easing commuter traffic and enhancing urban mobility.
• The underwater stretch beneath the Hooghly River is a marvel of engineering, utilizing advanced tunneling technology and safety measures.
• This project is a testament to India's growing capabilities in modern infrastructure development and urban transport solutions.
• The expansion is expected to boost economic activity along the corridor and reduce carbon emissions by promoting public transport.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Sustainable Growth in Fisheries Sector

• The policy focuses on promoting 'Blue Revolution' initiatives, modernizing fishing infrastructure, and ensuring welfare of fishing communities.
• Key provisions include incentives for deep-sea fishing, aquaculture expansion, value chain development, and adoption of environmentally friendly practices.
• Special emphasis is laid on integrating technology, such as satellite-based advisories and cold chain facilities, to improve efficiency and reduce post-harvest losses.
• The initiative seeks to boost seafood exports, create employment opportunities, and ensure food and nutritional security for the nation.

articles.headline: Ganga River Dolphin Conservation Project Shows Promising Results in Uttar Pradesh
articles.explainer:
• The ongoing Ganga River Dolphin Conservation Project in Uttar Pradesh has reported promising results, with an increase in population estimates in key stretches of the Ganga river.
• The project focuses on habitat protection, reducing anthropogenic pressures, community awareness, and tackling illegal poaching.
• The Ganga River Dolphin, India's National Aquatic Animal, is an endangered species primarily threatened by pollution, habitat fragmentation, and accidental catch.
• Conservation efforts include regular surveys, establishment of dolphin sanctuaries, and engagement with local communities for sustainable river management.
• The positive trend highlights the effectiveness of concerted conservation strategies and the importance of maintaining a healthy river ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Bio-diversity
Disaster and disaster management.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review: Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review of the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The review highlighted significant strides in curriculum reforms and integration of vocational education, but emphasized the need for accelerated progress in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
• New initiatives were announced to strengthen teacher training programs and enhance digital learning resources, especially in remote areas.
• The policy aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands, promoting critical thinking, creativity, and multidisciplinary learning.
• Challenges related to infrastructure, teacher-student ratios, and equitable access remain key areas for targeted intervention.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's 'Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0', consider the following statements:
1. The mission targets achieving 10 million tonnes per annum of Green Hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
2. It primarily focuses on establishing production hubs in coastal areas due to proximity to renewable energy sources.
3. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) program provides financial incentives for electrolyser manufacturing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the mission aims for 10 MTPA production capacity by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect as while coastal areas are important, the mission aims for five major production hubs which could be distributed based on various factors, not solely coastal proximity. Statement 3 is correct, the SIGHT program is a key component providing financial incentives.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the 'NavIC' system, recently enhanced by ISRO's NVS-03 launch, is/are correct?
1. NavIC is India's indigenous global positioning system, similar in coverage to GPS.
2. NVS-03 incorporates L1 and L5 band signals for enhanced accuracy.
3. NavIC primarily provides services for civilian users, with no strategic applications.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; NavIC is a regional navigation system, not global, covering India and its surrounding region. Statement 2 is correct, NVS-03 uses L1 and L5 band signals. Statement 3 is incorrect; NavIC provides both Standard Positioning Service (SPS) for civilian users and Restricted Service (RS) for strategic/authorized users.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Urban Heat Island (UHI)' effect, recently highlighted in Indian cities, refers to:
(a) The phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly lower temperatures than surrounding rural areas due to increased green cover.
(b) The phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly warmer temperatures than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and urban infrastructure.
(c) The impact of industrial pollution leading to localized temperature inversions in cities.
(d) The natural variation in temperature between different urban localities based on altitude.
options: (a) The phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly lower temperatures than surrounding rural areas due to increased green cover.
(b) The phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly warmer temperatures than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and urban infrastructure.
(c) The impact of industrial pollution leading to localized temperature inversions in cities.
(d) The natural variation in temperature between different urban localities based on altitude.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) The phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly warmer temperatures than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and urban infrastructure.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect describes urban areas being significantly warmer than their surrounding rural areas, primarily due to human activities, dark surfaces (asphalt, concrete) that absorb heat, and lack of vegetation.

3. It is primarily used by the Indian Navy for maritime surveillance.

prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, LCH 'Prachand' is indigenously developed by HAL. Statement 2 is correct, it is designed for high-altitude operations and incorporates stealth features. Statement 3 is incorrect, LCH 'Prachand' has been inducted into the Indian Air Force (IAF) and Indian Army, not primarily the Indian Navy.

GS1.headline: Urban Heat Island Effect Intensifies in Major Cities, Posing Public Health Risks
GS1.explainer:
• A recent study indicates that the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect is intensifying in major Indian cities, leading to significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas.
• This phenomenon is primarily driven by rapid urbanization, extensive use of heat-absorbing materials (concrete, asphalt), lack of green spaces, and anthropogenic heat emissions.
• The elevated temperatures pose serious public health risks, including heat strokes, respiratory illnesses, and increased energy consumption for cooling.
• Researchers advocate for sustainable urban planning strategies such as increasing green infrastructure, using cool pavements, and implementing urban forestry programs.
• Addressing UHI is crucial for climate resilience, improving urban livability, and achieving India's climate action goals.

Environmental pollution and degradation
Climate Change
Disaster and disaster management

mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the intensification of the Urban Heat Island effect in Indian cities and its environmental consequences.
Discuss the significant public health risks and socio-economic challenges posed by elevated urban temperatures.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various sustainable urban planning strategies and technological solutions in mitigating the UHI effect.

GS1.headline: ASI Unearths Significant Ancient Buddhist Monastery Complex in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a large, well-preserved ancient Buddhist monastery complex in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The excavation revealed multiple stupas, viharas, a prayer hall, and numerous artifacts including statues of Buddha and Bodhisattvas, indicating a thriving Buddhist center.
• Initial findings suggest the complex dates back to the 7th-10th centuries CE, providing valuable insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India.
• This discovery adds to Odisha's rich Buddhist heritage, alongside sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri, often referred to as the 'Diamond Triangle'.
• The site holds immense potential for historical research, cultural tourism, and understanding ancient trade routes and religious practices.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy in Landmark Data Protection Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a significant judgment, emphasized the inviolability of the Right to Privacy in the context of emerging data protection challenges.
• The ruling clarified certain ambiguities regarding data sharing between government agencies and private entities, stressing the need for explicit consent.
• It reiterated principles from the K.S. Puttaswamy judgment, affirming privacy as an intrinsic part of Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for the implementation of the proposed Digital Personal Data Protection Bill.
• Calls for a robust regulatory framework that balances national security concerns with individual data autonomy.

• The session also saw intense debates on issues ranging from economic policies to inter-state water disputes, highlighting the vibrancy of India's parliamentary democracy.
• Despite initial disruptions, the productivity of both Houses remained satisfactory, with significant legislative business being conducted.
• The government emphasized its commitment to inclusive development and strengthening social safety nets through these new laws.

GS2.headline: India's Progress Towards SDGs: Report Identifies Key Challenges and Opportunities
GS2.explainer:
• A new report released by NITI Aayog provides an updated assessment of India's progress towards achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by 2030.
• While significant strides have been made in areas like poverty reduction (SDG 1) and access to clean energy (SDG 7), challenges persist in health (SDG 3), education (SDG 4), and gender equality (SDG 5).
• The report highlights regional disparities in SDG achievement, with certain states lagging behind national averages, necessitating targeted interventions.
• It emphasizes the critical role of robust data collection, multi-stakeholder partnerships, and innovative financing mechanisms to accelerate progress.
• Recommendations include strengthening decentralized planning, enhancing social protection, and investing in green technologies to meet the ambitious 2030 agenda.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0': Ambitious Targets Set for Energy Transition
GS3.explainer:
• The second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish five major Green Hydrogen production hubs across India.
• Targets include achieving 10 million tonnes per annum of Green Hydrogen production capacity by 2030, attracting significant investments, and creating 6 lakh jobs.
• Focus on developing indigenous manufacturing capabilities for electrolysers and associated supply chain components, boosting 'Make in India' in the energy sector.
• New incentives for R&D in green hydrogen technologies and pilot projects for diverse end-use sectors like steel, mobility, and shipping.
• The mission seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.

GS3.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate Steady Amidst Inflationary Concerns, Growth Projections
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the benchmark repo rate at 6.5%.
• The decision reflects a cautious stance amidst lingering global inflationary pressures and volatile crude oil prices, despite domestic inflation showing signs of moderation.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth for FY 2025-26 at 7.0%, while retail inflation is expected to average 4.8%.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is prioritizing price stability, waiting for durable signs of disinflation before considering rate cuts.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Light Combat Helicopter 'Prachand' Fully Inducted into IAF Fleet
GS3.explainer:
• The Light Combat Helicopter (LCH) 'Prachand', developed by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), has been fully inducted into the Indian Air Force (IAF).
• This marks a significant milestone for India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in defense manufacturing and reduces reliance on foreign imports.
• The LCH is an all-weather, agile attack helicopter designed for high-altitude operations, anti-insurgency, and combat search & rescue roles.
• It features advanced stealth characteristics, armored protection, night attack capability, and a robust weapon suite.
• The induction enhances the IAF's combat capabilities, particularly in challenging terrains like the Himalayas, bolstering national security.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Gen Navigation Satellite NVS-03, Enhancing Regional Positioning
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched NVS-03, a next-generation navigation satellite, aboard the GSLV Mk-II rocket from Sriharikota.
• NVS-03 is part of India's Navigation with Indian Constellation (NavIC) system, designed to provide accurate real-time positioning and timing services over India and its surrounding region.
• This satellite incorporates advanced L1 and L5 band signals, enhancing accuracy and availability for both civilian and strategic users.
• The launch further strengthens India's self-reliance in space-based navigation and reduces dependence on foreign GPS systems.
• NavIC applications include disaster management, vehicle tracking, precise timing, and mapping services, contributing to various socio-economic sectors.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework to Boost Critical Mineral Exploration and Production in India
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines launched a new comprehensive policy framework aimed at accelerating the exploration and production of critical minerals in India.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for private sector participation, advanced geological surveys, and streamlined auction processes for mineral blocks.
• Critical minerals like Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements are crucial for India's energy transition, electric vehicle manufacturing, and high-tech industries.
• The framework emphasizes sustainable mining practices, environmental protection, and ensuring fair compensation for local communities.
• India seeks to reduce its import dependence on these strategic minerals and build a resilient domestic supply chain, bolstering economic security.

GS3.headline: Government Releases Comprehensive Guidelines for Ethical AI Development and Governance
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released a comprehensive set of guidelines for the ethical development and responsible governance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The guidelines emphasize principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, privacy, and non-discrimination in AI systems.
• They aim to foster innovation in AI while mitigating potential risks such as algorithmic bias, job displacement, and misuse of AI technologies.
• The framework proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for effective AI regulation.
• This move positions India as a leader in developing a balanced approach to AI, promoting its benefits while addressing ethical and societal concerns.

GS3.headline: India's First Underwater Metro Expands Operations in Kolkata
GS3.explainer:
• Kolkata's East-West Metro Line, featuring India's first underwater metro tunnel, announced expansion of its operational services.
• The extended route will connect more key areas of the city, significantly easing commuter traffic and enhancing urban mobility.
• The underwater stretch beneath the Hooghly River is a marvel of engineering, utilizing advanced tunneling technology and safety measures.
• This project is a testament to India's growing capabilities in modern infrastructure development and urban transport solutions.
• The expansion is expected to boost economic activity along the corridor and reduce carbon emissions by promoting public transport.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Sustainable Growth in Fisheries Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying launched a comprehensive new policy aimed at enhancing sustainable growth and productivity in India's fisheries sector.
• The policy focuses on promoting 'Blue Revolution' initiatives, modernizing fishing infrastructure, and ensuring welfare of fishing communities.
• Key provisions include incentives for deep-sea fishing, aquaculture expansion, value chain development, and adoption of environmentally friendly practices.
• Special emphasis is laid on integrating technology, such as satellite-based advisories and cold chain facilities, to improve efficiency and reduce post-harvest losses.
• The initiative seeks to boost seafood exports, create employment opportunities, and ensure food and nutritional security for the nation.

GS3.headline: Ganga River Dolphin Conservation Project Shows Promising Results in Uttar Pradesh
GS3.explainer:
• The ongoing Ganga River Dolphin Conservation Project in Uttar Pradesh has reported promising results, with an increase in population estimates in key stretches of the Ganga river.
• The project focuses on habitat protection, reducing anthropogenic pressures, community awareness, and tackling illegal poaching.
• The Ganga River Dolphin, India's National Aquatic Animal, is an endangered species primarily threatened by pollution, habitat fragmentation, and accidental catch.
• Conservation efforts include regular surveys, establishment of dolphin sanctuaries, and engagement with local communities for sustainable river management.
• The positive trend highlights the effectiveness of concerted conservation strategies and the importance of maintaining a healthy river ecosystem.
